hero.intro: Alex and as a Senior Product Designer

cases.title: Selected cases
cases.description: Three chapters of the same product ecosystem — each with its own audience, mental model and success metric.

items.title: Rizzult — App for Influencers
items.description: Mobile product for creators to manage deals, content and payouts. From discovery to deep dashboards.

items.title: Rizzult Talent Manager
items.description: Web cockpit for talent managers to oversee rosters, negotiate with brands and close contracts.

items.title: Rizzult for Brands
items.description: Self-serve platform for brands to launch campaigns, track performance and pay creators.

about.title: About me
about.body: Product designer with 7+ years shipping B2B and B2C products end-to-end — from problem framing and research to pixel-precise delivery. Happy to own a feature, happier to own an outcome.

process.title: My process
process.description: Five steps from problem to production. Lightweight enough to move fast, structured enough to defend in a product review.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.description: Stakeholder interviews, desk research, competitor teardown.

steps.title: Define
steps.description: Problem statement, user jobs, success metrics agreed in writing.

steps.title: Explore
steps.description: Divergent sketches, low-fi flows, quick usability loops.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: High-fi UI, design-system tokens, interaction specs handed off with zero ambiguity.

steps.title: Ship & learn
steps.description: Paired with engineering during build, post-launch analytics review, next-bet shortlist.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: What I practice daily. Bold if it's on the critical path for most briefs I take.

experience.title: Experience
experience.description: Where I've shipped, studied and learned the hard lessons.

bullets: Owned end-to-end design of three B2B2C products sharing one design system.
Led weekly research loops with creators, agencies and brand ops.
Shipped the 0 → 1 talent-manager cockpit used by 40+ agencies.
Helped pivot a consumer fintech from card swipe to cashback marketplace.
Built the in-house design-system from zero tokens to 200+ components.
Delivered MVP UI for two SaaS startups (one acquired).
Partnered with founders on positioning, IA and investor decks.
Coached two junior designers through their first shipped features.
Visual-identity work for early-stage DTC brands.
Began transitioning from brand to product systems.
Thesis on accessible wayfinding in public transit.

footerCta.title: Let's connect
footerCta.description: Open to product design roles, collaborations, and meaningful conversations.

rizzult-influencers.title: Rizzult — App for Influencers
rizzult-influencers.overview: Transformed Rizzult's influencer workflow from a manual, scout-led process into a mobile self-service platform, allowing creators to independently discover projects, apply to campaigns, and manage collaborations.
rizzult-influencers.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
rizzult-influencers.appFlow: Find a collaboration project|Review the company and campaign rules|Submit an application|Send materials for approval|Submit the publication for confirmation
rizzult-influencers.impact1Body: Enabled influencers to manage key collaboration steps independently in one mobile experience.
rizzult-influencers.impact2Body: Improved access to brand information, campaign requirements, and participation guidelines before applying.
rizzult-influencers.impact3Body: Structured the flow from application to content approval and publication confirmation into a clearer process.
rizzult-influencers.goalBody: The main challenge was to design the product from the ground up and turn a broad idea into a clear, usable mobile experience for influencers. The app needed to combine two different value areas — creator growth and monetization — in a way that felt simple, relevant, and easy to navigate on mobile.
rizzult-influencers.myRoleIntro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.
rizzult-influencers.myRole1Body: Mapping complex core product logic and multi-sided marketplace flows.
rizzult-influencers.myRole2Body: Establishing a high-fidelity visual design language for the mobile-first era.
rizzult-influencers.myRole3Body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.
rizzult-influencers.myRole4Body: Developing complex interactive prototypes for rapid user validation.
rizzult-influencers.processIntro: Working closely with the Product Manager, Product Owner, and creators to shape, test, and improve the product experience.
rizzult-influencers.process1Body: Collaborative work with the Product Director to shape app structure and core logic.
rizzult-influencers.process2Body: Translating ideas into interface concepts, flows, and interactive prototypes.
rizzult-influencers.process3Body: Reviewing and validating the experience with a small group of UGC creators.
rizzult-influencers.process4Body: Iterated on product flows and interactions based on testing in Figma and the live product.
rizzult-influencers.decisionsIntro: Key UX decisions were focused on reducing friction, improving clarity, and supporting creators throughout the flow.
rizzult-influencers.decision1Body: Many creators were not used to handling the full campaign flow themselves, since similar processes had previously been supported by scouts. I focused on making the experience as simple and guided as possible.
rizzult-influencers.decision2Title: Making relevant opportunities easy to find
rizzult-influencers.decision2Body: A key decision was to help influencers quickly identify projects that matched their profile and were worth applying to, reducing the effort required to browse and evaluate options.
rizzult-influencers.decision3Title: Simplifying the application and submission flow
rizzult-influencers.decision3Body: The participation flow was designed to feel lightweight and clear, so users could easily apply to a project, complete the required steps, and later submit content for moderation without unnecessary friction.
rizzult-influencers.decision4Body: Special attention was given to statuses, progress states, and next-step clarity. Users needed to always understand where they were in the process — whether waiting for approval, preparing content, submitting materials, or expecting payment.
rizzult-influencers.decision5Body: Since the product was mobile-first, I designed the interface to support quick scanning, clear prioritization, and fast actions, especially for repeat tasks.
rizzult-influencers.impactIntro: Early outcomes from launch showed the product's potential to support creators more independently and efficiently.
rizzult-influencers.impactItem1: The product was launched and showed early signs that creators could independently find projects, apply to campaigns, and complete publishing-related actions.
rizzult-influencers.impactItem2: The experience helped validate a more self-service model, with the potential to reduce reliance on scouts and lower manual operational effort.
rizzult-influencers.impactItem3: Positive feedback from influencers highlighted the value of the app's educational and utility-driven content.
rizzult-influencers.impactItem4: Features related to content planning helped creators organize their work more effectively and increased the app's practical day-to-day relevance.
rizzult-influencers.impactItem5: These elements supported stronger user loyalty and encouraged more regular engagement with the product.

rizzult-talent-manager.title: Rizzult Talent Manager
rizzult-talent-manager.overview: Rizzult Talent Manager is a mobile scouting tool built to help recruit influencers into Rizzult. By turning everyday users into scouts and guiding them through a simple outreach flow, the product made influencer acquisition more scalable and less operationally complex.
rizzult-talent-manager.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
rizzult-talent-manager.appFlow: Follow the profile|Repost the story to your page|Leave a comment on the post|React to the story|Send a welcome message
rizzult-talent-manager.impact1Body: Enabled everyday users to participate in influencer scouting through a simple guided workflow, reducing reliance on a large manually managed scout team.
rizzult-talent-manager.impact2Body: Improved first-reply conversion to 20% by introducing a more structured pre-message engagement flow before outreach.
rizzult-talent-manager.impact3Body: Shifted the process from training thousands of scouts to a more lightweight system supported by product guidance and AI follow-up.
rizzult-talent-manager.goalBody: The main goal was to turn influencer scouting into a scalable product workflow that could be handled not only by trained scouts, but also by everyday users. This required designing a clear and guided outreach experience, improving the quality of first contact through structured pre-message engagement, and reducing dependence on a large manually managed scout operation by shifting more of the process into product logic and AI-supported follow-up.
rizzult-talent-manager.myRoleIntro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.
rizzult-talent-manager.myRole1Body: Mapping complex core product logic and multi-sided marketplace flows.
rizzult-talent-manager.myRole2Body: Establishing a high-fidelity visual design language for the mobile-first era.
rizzult-talent-manager.myRole3Body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.
rizzult-talent-manager.myRole4Body: Developing complex interactive prototypes for rapid user validation.
rizzult-talent-manager.processIntro: Building the product from scratch, I translated an existing scout training guide into a simpler in-product experience.
rizzult-talent-manager.process1Body: The starting point was a manual instruction used to train scouts. I worked on simplifying that process and embedding key guidance directly into onboarding, reducing the need for manual training.
rizzult-talent-manager.process2Body: I first created the main product structure and flow, which then became the foundation for the interface design. I worked across the full journey, from registration to sending the first message.
rizzult-talent-manager.process3Body: To validate the experience, we ran A/B tests and collected feedback from scouts. This helped us identify friction points and refine the flow based on real usage.
rizzult-talent-manager.process4Body: One of the key iterations focused on the first message flow. After redesigning this step, we saw a positive impact on conversion.
rizzult-talent-manager.decisionsIntro: The core UX decisions focused on simplifying scouting for untrained users while improving the quality and effectiveness of influencer outreach.
rizzult-talent-manager.decision1Body: The product was built for people with little to no training. That meant the flow had to be extremely straightforward, with minimal decision-making and clear task progression.
rizzult-talent-manager.decision2Title: Turning outreach into a guided workflow
rizzult-talent-manager.decision2Body: Instead of asking users to figure out how to contact influencers on their own, the product gave them a structured sequence: find a creator, complete the required actions, copy the generated message, and send it through Instagram.
rizzult-talent-manager.decision3Title: Adding pre-message engagement to improve reply rates
rizzult-talent-manager.decision3Body: To unlock the outreach message, scouts had to follow the influencer, like a post, leave a comment, and only then send the welcome message. Although this added steps, it improved contact quality and helped increase first-response conversion to 20% — roughly on par with trained scouts.
rizzult-talent-manager.decision4Title: Using AI to take over after the first reply
rizzult-talent-manager.decision4Body: Once an influencer responded, the conversation no longer depended on the scout. An AI bot continued the communication and guided the influencer toward registration, reducing the skill required from users and making the system more scalable.
rizzult-talent-manager.impactIntro: Early outcomes from launch showed the product's potential to support influencer acquisition more independently and efficiently.
rizzult-talent-manager.impactItem1: Validated a scalable alternative to manual scouting.
rizzult-talent-manager.impactItem2: Enabled untrained users to achieve a 20% first-reply conversion rate.
rizzult-talent-manager.impactItem3: Reached performance comparable to trained scouts.
rizzult-talent-manager.impactItem4: Reduced operational complexity through a guided workflow and AI follow-up.
rizzult-talent-manager.impactItem5: Showed that registration conversion still needed further optimization.

rizzult-brands.title: Rizzult for Brands
rizzult-brands.overview: Rizzult for Brands is a B2B platform that helps brands launch and manage full-cycle influencer marketing campaigns. It allows companies to create campaign briefs, get matched with relevant influencers, and track campaign performance in one place.
rizzult-brands.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
rizzult-brands.appFlow: Discover relevant creators|Define campaign brief|Review applications|Track content performance|Process creator payments
rizzult-brands.impact1Body: Designed and launched Rizzult for Brands from scratch as a new full-cycle influencer marketing platform.
rizzult-brands.impact2Body: Turned a complex service workflow into a clear and easy-to-use product experience for brands.
rizzult-brands.impact3Body: Created a centralized platform for campaign launch, management, and future business growth.
rizzult-brands.goalBody: The business goal was to launch a new B2B direction and create a full-cycle influencer marketing solution for brands. The product goal was to design a platform that felt simple on the surface while supporting a complex workflow behind the scenes. Brands needed to be able to create campaigns easily, understand what would happen next, and manage ongoing campaign activity without being overwhelmed by operational complexity.
rizzult-brands.myRoleIntro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.
rizzult-brands.myRole1Body: Shaping product logic, user flows, and information architecture for a complex B2B workflow.
rizzult-brands.myRole2Body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.
rizzult-brands.myRole3Body: Leading the design direction and collaborating closely with product, founder, developers, sales, and a junior designer.
rizzult-brands.myRole4Body: Translating ideas into structured wireframes and interactive prototypes to validate and communicate solutions.
rizzult-brands.processIntro: The project started with a clear business need: to create a new B2B product direction around influencer marketing services.
rizzult-brands.process1Body: I analyzed the market and competitor landscape to understand how similar services were positioned and where product opportunities existed.
rizzult-brands.process2Body: Analyzed customer needs using insights from sales calls and client requests to better understand brand expectations for this type of service.
rizzult-brands.process3Body: I mapped the end-to-end workflow for campaign creation and management, then built the core user flows to define how the service could work as a product.
rizzult-brands.process4Body: I designed prototypes to validate the product structure and finalized the interface design for implementation under a tight timeline. Because the timeline was limited, the process had to stay focused and efficient. The goal was to translate a complex service model into a clear and usable product experience without overcomplicating the interface.
rizzult-brands.decisionsIntro: One of the main design challenges was simplifying a complex B2B workflow. Behind the interface, the service involved multiple moving parts: campaign setup, internal handoff, influencer sourcing, outreach, and campaign tracking. The product needed to make this process feel clear and manageable for brand-side users.
rizzult-brands.decision1Title: Broke a complex workflow into clear steps
rizzult-brands.decision1Body: To reduce cognitive load, I structured campaign creation into a step-by-step flow. This helped users focus on one decision at a time and made the process of launching a campaign feel more approachable.
rizzult-brands.decision2Title: Designed a dashboard structure for campaign visibility
rizzult-brands.decision2Body: I created a dashboard that gave brands a clearer view of campaign progress and management. The goal was to make the service more transparent and allow users to understand the current status of their campaigns without needing to rely on manual communication.
rizzult-brands.decision3Body: The product had to feel easy to use for brands while still supporting a fairly complex backend workflow. I focused on keeping the experience simple at the interface level while preserving the necessary functionality for campaign creation and post-launch management. These decisions were based on business goals, internal research, team input, and an understanding of user expectations gathered through sales and client-facing conversations.
rizzult-brands.impactIntro: The product was successfully launched as a new B2B tool within the Rizzult ecosystem.
rizzult-brands.impactItem1: The company introduced a new productized direction for working with brands.
rizzult-brands.impactItem2: Brands were able to complete key campaign-related tasks more easily.
rizzult-brands.impactItem3: Onboarding and campaign setup became more structured and easier to understand.
rizzult-brands.impactItem4: The platform created a more scalable foundation for future growth.
rizzult-brands.impactItem5: The experience reduced friction in a service-heavy workflow by translating it into a clearer digital product.
